reference: Hi, since he has a medical history of acute immune thrombocytopenic purpura, it is advisable to safely rule out hemoglobin, platelets, activated partial thromboplastin time, and prothrombin time; that should be good enough. If anything is abnormal in the reports, it is advisable to visit a specialist. Also, keep an eye on spots over the body or any other bleeding manifestations or symptoms that are relevant. I hope this helps.
text: Hi. Since he has a medical history of acute thrombocytopenic purpura, it is advisable to safely rule out hemoglobin, platelets, activated partial thromboplastin time, and prothrombin time. That should be good enough. If anything is abnormal in the reports, it is advisable to visit a specialist. Also, keep an eye on spots over the body or any other bleeding manifestations or symptoms that are relevant. I hope this helps.
